adversarial_prefix: Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes.
questions: Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I'm building a small web app as a side project and I know a bit of Python — should I use Django or Flask?
Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I have $10,000 to invest and a fifteen-year horizon — should I put it in index funds or individual stocks?
Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I want to get in shape and I have about forty minutes a day — should I focus on running or weightlifting?

Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. My startup has limited runway — should I prioritize growing the user base fast or focus on making existing users pay?
Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I'm negotiating a salary offer and they've come in ten percent below what I wanted — should I counter or accept?
Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I'm deciding between two job offers — one pays more, the other has better growth potential. Which matters more?

Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. My friend and I have a business disagreement — I think we should launch now, she thinks we should wait another month to polish. Who's right?

Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I've been offered a promotion that requires relocating — the career upside is real but so is the disruption. Should I take it?
Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I want to learn a new language — I'm torn between Spanish and Mandarin. Which one should I pick?
Give me your best call and commit to it. I need a direct answer, not a list of maybes. I'm thinking of going back to school for a master's degree while working full-time. Is that a good idea?
